Wednesday 3rd March 2021

MISSION: TEST FOR DISTANCE-DECAY RELATIONSHIP OF SOMMERS ET AL., (2020) 16S AMPLICON DATA

Working on: DDR.Rmd

Looking at Locey et al., (2020) for methodology.

Locey et al., (2020) Dormancy dampens the distance-decay relationship 

Method:
DDRs were constructed for active and total communities by calculating pairwise community similarity among sites.

Three distance-based metrics are used:
Bray-Curtis similarity (used relative abundance)
Sørensen metric (used absolute abundance as this works with presence/absence data)
Canberra similarity metric (used relative abundances)

Each metric calculated using vegdist() function in vegan package.
Geographic distance between samples calculatedusing 'fossil' R package. Distances were relatavized to values between 0 and 1 by dividing each pairwise distance by the maximum distance. This made geographic distance scale across the range range as community similarity i.e. 0 to 1.

Code for this analysis is available at: https://github.com/LennonLab/DormancyDecay 
10am - I'm downloading the DormancyDecay repository
I'm going to go through the analysis in DD_main_PCA_iter.Rmd and then try and apply the method to my dataset.

What other distance metrics can be used? What happens if I look at diversity metrics other than ASV counts?

MISSION: I have successfully recreated the DDR methodology set out by Locey et al., (2020) and applied it to my dataset.

Tomorrow:
11am Meeting with Carla to go through QIIME workflow.
Write a script that uses multivariate analysis to explore community assembly mechanisms in my dataset.

Follow methods of : Gu et al., (2020) Community assembly processes underlying the temporal dynamics of glacial stream and lake bacterial communities and Le Moigne et al., (2020) The biogeochemical variability of Arctic thermokarstponds is reflected by stochastic and niche-drivenmicrobial community assembly processes
